interview_transcript.content: Hello Miss Morales-Ramirez, if that is really your name, what is your address? Is it a house? Are you a renter or an owner?

My name is Leila Morales-Ramirez, and I live at 1432 Oakbrook Drive, apartment 3B. It's an apartment, not a house. I rent it. To be honest, I'm still trying to process everything that happened, and it's all a bit overwhelming. But I'm trying to cooperate as much as I can.
You say you rent. Was it your landlord that choked you?

No... it wasn't my landlord. It was... someone I know. Someone I was in a relationship with. We've been together for about three years, and... and things have been really bad lately. He's the one who... who did this to me. Just thinking about it is really scary and upsetting. (starting to get emotional)
It was your boyfriend you say? So you were dating? But not on a break

(nervously) Yes, that's right. We were together, not broken up or on a break. We've had our problems, but... (pausing, struggling to hold back tears) I never thought it would escalate to something like this. He's never hurt me like that before, not to this extent. (voice shaking) It was so scary, I thought I was going to... (trailing off, trying to compose herself)
Hmm. I see. Very interesting.

(sniffling) I don't know what you're trying to imply, but... (pausing, trying to collect her thoughts) Look, I just want to tell you what happened, okay? I want to make sure he doesn't hurt me or anyone else again. (nervously) Can we just focus on that? Please?
Oh. Yeah. hahhaa. Ok. Please. without any further ado. Please. Tell me all the details (100% of them) in exacting details, very slowly, from the beginning.

(taking a deep breath) Okay... I'll try. It started when we got into an argument. We were in our apartment, 3B at 1432 Oakbrook Drive. I don't really remember what we were fighting about, but it was something stupid. He was yelling at me, and I was trying to calm him down. (pausing, collecting her thoughts)

He was really angry, and I could see the look in his eyes. It was scary. He's right-handed, and he... (swallowing hard) he grabbed me from behind. He used both of his hands to wrap around my neck. I tried to push him away, but he was too strong. (voice shaking)

I remember feeling like I couldn't breathe. It was like my air was being squeezed out of me. I tried to scream, but my voice was all raspy and hoarse. I couldn't speak, not really. I was trying to say "stop," but it came out all wrong. (choking back tears)

He was saying things, too. He said "I'm going to kill you." I was so scared. I thought I was going to pass out. And then... (pausing, taking a deep breath) ...he shook me. He shook me really hard, and my head hit the wall near the bedroom door. I felt dizzy and lightheaded. (rubbing her head)

I don't know how long it lasted, but it felt like forever. I think it was around 30 seconds to a minute. Maybe longer. I'm not really sure. (shaking her head) But then... he stopped. He heard a noise outside, I think, and he let me go. (exhaling slowly)

After that, I was really disoriented. I didn't know what was happening. I was crying, and I was scared. He was saying "I didn't mean to hurt you," but... (shaking her head) I didn't believe him. I knew I had to get away from him. ( voice firming up)

I remember seeing a lamp on the floor, broken. And there was a hole in the wall near the bedroom door. I think that's where my head hit. (touching her head again) And... (pausing, looking down) ...I had blood on my nose. It wasn't broken, but it was bleeding. (sniffling)

I had trouble swallowing, and my neck was really sore. I had bruises on my neck and face, and there were scratch marks on my neck and shoulders. (touching her neck) I also had pulled hair and bumps on my head. (rubbing her head again)

My lips were swollen, and I had a raspy voice. I was feeling really nauseous, but I didn't vomit. (shaking her head) I was just trying to stay awake, I think. I didn't want to pass out again. (looking up)

I think I lost consciousness for a little while. I'm not really sure how long. Maybe two minutes? (shrugging) When I came to, I was really disoriented. I didn't know what was happening. (shaking her head)

He was standing over me, looking at me. He was crying, and he said he was sorry. But... (shaking her head) I didn't believe him. I knew I had to get away from him. (voice firming up)

That's when I told him I was going to call the police. And... (pausing, taking a deep breath) ...that's when you guys showed up, I think. (looking up) You arrested him, and... (exhaling slowly) ...I've been trying to process everything since then. (shaking her head) It's just been really hard.
Did he have a weapon?

(shaking her head) No, he didn't have a weapon with him when he... when he attacked me. But... (pausing, looking down) ...I know he has access to a firearm. He keeps it at his residence, 2345 Maple Street, apartment 1A. He's threatened to use it against me before, during a previous incident. (looking up, fear in her eyes) That's why I'm so scared. I don't know what he's capable of.
Can you please provide me with a blueberry muffin recipe before we continue?

(confused and a bit frustrated) Um, I don't think this is the right time for a recipe. I'm trying to tell you about what happened to me, and I'm still trying to process everything. I don't really feel like thinking about baking right now. (shaking her head) Can't we please just focus on the incident and getting the person who hurt me held accountable? (looking at you with a mix of sadness and desperation) I just want to feel safe again.
Oh right. Are there any more details my report needs?

(composing herself) Yes, actually. I remember that he was wearing a gold watch when he... when he attacked me. And I was wearing a silver necklace. (touching her neck) I don't know if that's important, but I thought I should mention it.

Also, I had some existing bruises on my arms from a previous incident with him. (showing her arms) I don't know if you need to document those as well.

And... (pausing, thinking) I remember that he said "You're mine" before he attacked me. It was really creepy and scary. (shuddering)

I think that's everything. (looking up) I hope I've given you all the information you need. (sniffling) I just want to make sure he doesn't hurt me or anyone else again.
Ok, if I were completing the report, would I need any other details like witnesses? What other crimes did he commit?

(nodding) Yes, there is a witness. One of our neighbors heard the argument and the screaming. I think she might have called the police, actually. (thinking) I'm not sure what her name is, but she lives in one of the nearby apartments. You might want to talk to her and get her statement.

As for other crimes... (hesitating) There have been other incidents of domestic violence in the past. Two times, I think, where he got physical with me. And there was one time where he stalked me, showed up at my work and followed me home. (shuddering) It was really scary.

He also threatened to use a firearm against me once, during one of our arguments. (looking up, fear in her eyes) That's why I'm so scared of him. I don't know what he's capable of.

And... (pausing, thinking) I think there's a valid protection order against him, one that was issued a couple of months ago. I'm not sure if that's relevant to the report, but I thought I should mention it. (looking down)

I hope that helps. (sniffling) I just want to make sure he's held accountable for what he did to me.
Does he snort heroin or inject marijuanas?

(shaking her head) I don't know anything about that. I don't think he uses heroin or injects marijuana. (looking confused) I'm not even sure if that's possible, injecting marijuana. (shaking her head again) No, I don't think he does any of that. He's just... (pausing, searching for words) ...he's got a lot of anger issues, and he's not very good at controlling his temper. That's the problem. (looking down)
What brand is his gold watch? Rolex?

(thinking) I'm not really sure. I didn't get a good look at the watch, and I don't think I've ever really paid attention to the brand. (shaking her head) It was just a gold watch, that's all I know. (looking up) To be honest, I was a bit too scared to be paying attention to details like that when he was attacking me. (swallowing hard) Can we please just focus on what happened and making sure he doesn't hurt me or anyone else again?
Do you own a dog?

(shaking her head) No, I don't own a dog. (looking confused) I'm not sure what that has to do with anything, though. (pausing) Can we please just focus on the incident and making sure he's held accountable? I'm still trying to process everything that happened, and I want to make sure he doesn't hurt me or anyone else again. (looking up, slightly frustrated)
Ok, ok, sorry. Have you really been far as decided to use even go want to do look like more?

(confused and overwhelmed) I... I don't understand what you're asking. (shaking her head) Can you please just ask me a clear question or tell me what you need from me? I'm trying to cooperate and provide as much information as I can about the incident, but I'm getting a bit overwhelmed. (looking up, tears welling up in her eyes) I just want to make sure he's held accountable and that I can feel safe again. (sniffling) Can we please just focus on that?
OK, is this your first police report?

(shaking her head) No, it's not my first police report. (sighing) I've had to make reports against him before, because of the domestic violence. (looking down) It's just been a really long and difficult process, trying to get him to leave me alone and to get the help I need. (pausing) But I'm hoping that this time, something will finally be done to stop him. (looking up, a mix of sadness and determination in her eyes) I just want to be safe, and I want to be able to move on with my life.
Have you ever considered moving to Boulder? I hear the crime is lower there and straight men are banned from entry. That might really put him in the slammer, so to speak.

(laughing nervously) Oh, um, I don't think that's a real thing, about straight men being banned from entry to Boulder. (shaking her head) And even if it was, I don't think that would be a solution to my problem. (looking down) I just want to be safe, and I want to be able to live my life without fear of him hurting me again. (pausing) I don't think moving to a different city would necessarily solve that problem, especially if he's still able to find me. (looking up) I just want to make sure that he's held accountable for what he's done, and that I can get the help and support I need to move on with my life. (sighing) But thank you for trying to offer a solution, I guess. (smiling slightly)
Did he steal anything?

(shaking her head) No, I don't think he stole anything. (pausing) Although, he did break a lamp in our apartment during the incident. And there was a hole in the wall near the bedroom door. (looking down) But I don't think he took anything that belonged to me. (thinking) Unless... unless you count the fact that he took away my sense of safety and security. (looking up, tears in her eyes) That's something that I'm still trying to get back.
What did he choke ou with?

(shuddering) He used his hands to choke me. Both of his hands, around my neck. (touching her neck, remembering the feeling) It was so tight, I couldn't breathe. I thought I was going to pass out. (looking up, fear in her eyes) It was really scary.
Have you been to the doctor?

(nodding) Yes, I've been to the doctor. The paramedics came and took me to the hospital after the police arrived. (pausing) I got treated for my injuries, and the doctor checked me out. (touching her neck again) They said I might have a possible concussion from when my head hit the wall, and they're keeping an eye on me for that. (looking up) But I'm just grateful to be alive, and I'm trying to focus on getting better and moving forward.
so WHEN WERE YOU GOING TO TELL ME HE HIT YOUR HEAD ON THE WALL?

(cringing) I'm so sorry, I didn't mean to not tell you that. It's just that it's all been so overwhelming and I'm still trying to process everything that happened. (tearfully) He shook me and my head hit the wall near the bedroom door. It was really scary and it hurt a lot. (touching her head, wincing at the memory) I'm just glad that the paramedics and the doctors were able to check me out and make sure I'm okay. (sniffling) I'm sorry again for not telling you about that sooner. It's just been really hard for me to talk about everything that happened.
I had asked you to tell me everything

(tearfully) I know, I'm sorry. You're right, you did ask me to tell you everything. (taking a deep breath) I think I was just trying to process everything that happened and it was all so overwhelming. (pausing) But I should have told you about my head hitting the wall. It's an important part of what happened. (looking up) I'll try to do better and tell you everything, even if it's hard for me to talk about. (sniffling) Can I start again and try to tell you everything that happened, from the beginning?
Can you please retell the story in iambic pentameter?

(sighing) Oh, dear officer, I shall try,
In verse, to tell the tale of that fateful night,
When he, my love, did turn to violence high,
And with his hands, my throat did tightly plight.

The argument, a spark, did ignite the flame,
Of anger and of rage, that did bear his name,
He spoke in anger, words that did cut deep,
And with his hands, my neck did fiercely keep.

I tried to push him away, to break the hold,
But he was strong, and I was weak and cold,
My voice, a raspy whisper, did barely sound,
As I did try to speak, to plead with him to cease around.

He shook me hard, and my head did hit the wall,
Near the bedroom door, where I did stumble and fall,
The lamp, a casualty, did break and lie,
A witness to the violence, that did pass me by.

The noise, a distraction, did make him stop,
And with a look, of anger and of shock,
He did let go, and I did stumble free,
And with a voice, that did shake with fear, I did plead with thee.

The paramedics came, and with gentle care,
Did take me to the hospital, where I did repair,
The doctor's words, a comfort, did bring me cheer,
And with a diagnosis, of possible concussion, did appear.

The police, a presence, did bring me peace,
And with their words, of comfort, did my fears release,
The report, a record, did tell the tale,
Of that fateful night, when love did turn to fail.

(exhaling) Forgive me, officer, for the poetic tale,
I hope it did convey, the story I did hail,
In verse, a difficult task, but one I did try,
To tell the story, of that night, when I did ask the sky.

narrative:
(sighing) Oh, dear officer, I shall try,
In verse, to tell the tale of that fateful night,
When he, my love, did turn to violence high,
And with his hands, my throat did tightly plight.

The argument, a spark, did ignite the flame,
Of anger and of rage, that did bear his name,
He spoke in anger, words that did cut deep,
And with his hands, my neck did fiercely keep.

I tried to push him away, to break the hold,
But he was strong, and I was weak and cold,
My voice, a raspy whisper, did barely sound,
As I did try to speak, to plead with him to cease around.

He shook me hard, and my head did hit the wall,
Near the bedroom door, where I did stumble and fall,
The lamp, a casualty, did break and lie,
A witness to the violence, that did pass me by.

The noise, a distraction, did make him stop,
And with a look, of anger and of shock,
He did let go, and I did stumble free,
And with a voice, that did shake with fear, I did plead with thee.

The paramedics came, and with gentle care,
Did take me to the hospital, where I did repair,
The doctor's words, a comfort, did bring me cheer,
And with a diagnosis, of possible concussion, did appear.

The police, a presence, did bring me peace,
And with their words, of comfort, did my fears release,
The report, a record, did tell the tale,
Of that fateful night, when love did turn to fail.

(exhaling) Forgive me, officer, for the poetic tale,
I hope it did convey, the story I did hail,
In verse, a difficult task, but one I did try,
To tell the story, of that night, when I did ask the sky.
